# Work Order & Incident Report — DOC1135
**Document type:** Work Order / Incident Narrative (synthetic narrative record)
**Failure:** F1073 — Random Trip
**Equipment:** EQ-CR-02 — Overhead Crane (Rolling Mill Bay) #2 (Rajendra Steel Plant, Mumbai Unit - Rolling Mill Bay)
**Event timestamp:** 2026-02-05 08:10:09  |  **RCA:** RCA1073 (closed 2026-02-10)

## 1. Incident Narrative
At 08:10:09 on 2026-02-05, the Overhead Crane (Rolling Mill Bay) #2 raised a random trip alarm and was taken out of normal operation. Condition monitoring captured air temperature 301.3 K, process temperature 311.8 K, rotational speed 1580 rpm, torque 55.6 Nm and accumulated tool wear 5 minutes at the moment of the trip. The shift crew isolated the equipment under the plant permit-to-work system and raised this work order for investigation and repair.

## 2. Response & Diagnosis
Naveen Pillai (Shift A), Divya Banerjee (Shift B) attended the equipment and completed the make-safe checks before handover to the investigation team. The root cause analysis RCA1073, performed by Lakshmi Patel (quality inspector, Shift B), concluded: No single dominant cause was isolated; the event is classified as a random failure with low recurrence probability. Sensor traces were within normal bands prior to the trip, suggesting a transient or latent component issue.

## 3. Procedure Review
The investigation reviewed the governing procedure PROC-030 for this asset and recorded a compliance finding: Step 4 of PROC-030 ("Check sensor and instrumentation readings against the qualified operating envelope and record them in the shift log.") — the routine monitoring entry was missing from the shift log prior to the event. This finding is the actionable link between the maintenance routine and the failure mechanism, and it was logged so recurrence checks can compare like-for-like across the fleet.

## 4. Corrective Work Completed
Reset and returned equipment to service under enhanced monitoring; scheduled a follow-up inspection to confirm no developing fault. Parts and consumables used were booked to this work order, and post-repair verification confirmed the equipment returned to its qualified operating envelope before release back to production.

## 5. Closure
Work order closed on 2026-02-10 with the corrective action verified in service. This record is a synthetic narrative generated for the Synapse knowledge graph and is tagged accordingly; it mirrors the structure of plant work-order documentation for retrieval purposes. Linked records: failure F1073, RCA RCA1073, procedure PROC-030, equipment EQ-CR-02.
